posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: Great question. And honestly, this is the right risk to discuss. I don’t see PSB Xchange as a TReDS clone. TReDS solved supplier invoice liquidity. PSB Xchange, if executed well, can solve corporate ecosystem working capital. That is a wider and more difficult working-capital problem. First, the market itself is huge. India’s formal MSME credit outstanding is already around ₹31 lakh Cr , and the estimated MSME credit gap is another ~₹30 lakh Cr . Against this, FY25 TReDS throughput was roughly ₹2.3 lakh Cr . Approx FY25 TReDS reference split: RXIL: ~₹80,500 Cr, around 35% share M1xchange: ~₹78…

posts.text: Uday ji how big TReDS could be threat to VEEFIN ? what if RBI significantly expands TReDS to cover more than invoice discounting and allows to cover purchase order financing, Inventory financing , Broader cash-flow lending n other aspects ? How do you see it ?

posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: Veefin and PSB Xchange: Building the Working-Capital Rail Inside Corporate India A small business can have demand, customers and orders — and still struggle to make money. The reason is often simple: Working capital. Take a large company like RR Kabel, Shree Cement or Godrej . Around such companies sits a wide network of dealers, distributors, vendors and suppliers. Many of these businesses are small or mid-sized. They need money to buy stock, supply goods, wait for invoices and keep the cycle moving. Stock. Supply. Invoices. Repeat. But when input costs rise, the pressure becomes brutal. Raw…

posts.text: Yes, positive for sure. They were working on migration to mainboard genuinely by doing quarterly results and other formalities needed. Veefin listed on the stock market on July 5, 2023 . So 3 years by next month. Shareholder count is around 5000, minimum needed is 500 so no issues, minimum equity capital needed is 10cr. They will go through. Efforts are visible. Next month concall would be super interesting. Migration + merger + numbers of PSB as Mr Raja said fy27 is execution time, so market will watch eagerly because so far price action is mediocre. Price is not strong. Market is waiting for…

posts.text: IMG_2933 1170×1464 231 KB Positive hopefully Migration from BSE SME to BSE Main Board Direct listing on NSE Main Board Postal ballot approval from shareholders for the above Other business Veefin seems broadly eligible on size, capital, market cap and profitability. The remaining watch items are three-year listing timing, public shareholder count, exchange due diligence, compliance record, and final BSE/NSE approval.
